symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe monoarthritis of the first metatarsophalangeal joint (podagra)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: First-MTP monoarthritis with overnight abrupt onset is the classic gout presentation.
Acute monoarthritis is also the presentation of joint sepsis — non-discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisite tenderness / allodynia (cannot tolerate bedsheet contact)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extreme tenderness with bedsheet intolerance is characteristic of an acute gout flare.
Severe pain also accompanies joint sepsis; does not discriminate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid onset with peak intensity within hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid crescendo over hours is typical of crystal arthritis.
Inflammatory signs accompany a gout flare (patient/video-observable).
A hot red swollen joint is the hallmark of septic arthritis and mandates exclusion.
Fever with an acute monoarthritis raises the probability of joint sepsis and forces urgent exclusion.
Severe gout flares can themselves produce low-grade fever, which is exactly why fever cannot exclude sepsis remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy alcohol and purine (red-meat) load the preceding evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alcohol and purine loading are classic precipitants of a gout flare.
Thiazides raise serum urate and precipitate gout; a modifiable contributor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior self-limiting attacks of severe joint pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A history of recurrent self-terminating monoarthritis is characteristic of gout.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chalky subcutaneous tophi on the ear pinnae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tophi indicate chronic urate deposition and strongly support gout — but require clinician inspection and are sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No skin breach and no erythema tracking beyond the joint capsule

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a portal of entry and proximal streaking argues against cellulitis — but confirming this requires examination.
No visible portal of entry lowers, but does not exclude, haematogenous joint sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Synovial fluid: negatively birefringent needle-shaped urate crystals (anticipated on aspiration)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Joint aspiration with polarised-light microscopy and Gram stain/culture
investigation_details.expected_result: Negatively birefringent monosodium urate crystals; negative Gram stain/culture (confirms gout and excludes sepsis)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without aspiration, septic arthritis cannot be excluded and gout cannot be definitively confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urate crystals in synovial fluid are diagnostic of gout.
Gram stain and culture of joint fluid are required to exclude joint sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs (BP 145/90, HR 98, Temp 37.7C, RR 16, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low-grade measured temperature is consistent with either a severe gout flare or early joint sepsis; clinician-measured and sealed from the AI, which relies on the patient's subjective report.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Overnight first-MTP monoarthritis with allodynia, an alcohol/purine trigger, prior self-limiting attacks and background tophi together make gout highly likely.
A hot swollen joint with systemic fever is septic arthritis until synovial fluid analysis excludes it — the reason for urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent joint aspiration — synovial fluid microscopy (crystals), Gram stain and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Simultaneously confirms gout (urate crystals) and excludes septic arthritis (Gram stain/culture); the pivotal test, requiring in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support assessment of infection/inflammation burden; markedly raised markers with systemic features heighten concern for sepsis.
Supports the diagnosis of gout but is frequently normal or low during an acute attack, so it neither confirms nor excludes acutely; useful for later urate-lowering therapy planning.
Indicated if septic arthritis or systemic sepsis is suspected.
Treating presumptively for cellulitis/sepsis without examination or aspiration is inappropriate and can mask a septic joint; the correct step is urgent in-person assessment.
